mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, with Rank IC exceeding 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a combined Rank IC below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, which increases discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL rises, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during high inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and rising corporate profit margins, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with a mean forward return below zero during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 60% of months with payroll growth above 200,000, or the signal exhibits negative Rank IC over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, which boosts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations, invalidating the demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates falling below 3.0% indicate accommodative monetary policy that lowers corporate financing costs and stimulates equity investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain negative or flat when FEDFUNDS drops below 3.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.
